race.name: Marchablanca
race.tagline: Twenty-one kilometers of cross-country skiing at the bottom of the world near Ushuaia — South America's most important XC ski race since 1986, on the trails of Tierra del Fuego.

vitals.registration: Online registration via Club Andino Ushuaia

climate.description: August in Ushuaia at 54°S latitude means deep Southern Hemisphere winter. Temperatures range from -5°C to 5°C. Tierra del Fuego's maritime subantarctic climate provides consistent but often wet and windy conditions. Snow can be heavy and wind-affected. Daylight is limited — about 9 hours in August at this extreme southern latitude.
challenges: Subantarctic wind — Tierra del Fuego is notoriously windy
Wet maritime snow — heavier than continental powder
Remote southern location — limited international accessibility
Variable snow quality due to maritime influence

course.primary: A 21km course through the Tierra del Fuego landscape near Ushuaia, the world's southernmost city. The course traverses subantarctic forest and open terrain at approximately 300m altitude. Moderate rolling terrain with views of the Martial Glacier and Beagle Channel. A shorter 7.5km promotional distance is also offered.
course.surface: Groomed trails through subantarctic forest and open terrain
features: World's southernmost major XC ski race
Ushuaia — 'End of the World' city at 54°S
Views of Martial Glacier and Beagle Channel
South America's most important XC ski race since 1986

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Unique geographic position as the world's southernmost major XC ski race. Strong scenery in the Tierra del Fuego subantarctic landscape. International draw boosted by Worldloppet candidacy and southern hemisphere timing. Short distance and limited grooming infrastructure limit some scores.

history.summary: The Marchablanca has been South America's most important cross-country ski race since its founding in 1986. Organized by the Club Andino Ushuaia (CAU), a nonprofit institution nearly sixty years old, the race takes place in Tierra del Fuego near Ushuaia — the world's southernmost city. The event has been part of the Worldloppet candidacy process alongside the Ushuaia Loppet, seeking to bring South American citizen skiing into the global Worldloppet family. With up to 780 entries in its peak years, it represents the heart of Argentine cross-country skiing culture.
notable_facts: Founded in 1986 — South America's most important XC ski race
Organized by Club Andino Ushuaia (CAU) — nearly 60 years old
Ushuaia — the world's southernmost city at 54°S latitude
Worldloppet candidacy — seeking to join the global loppet family
Peak participation of 780 entries in the 21km distance
Southern Hemisphere timing — August racing during boreal summer
30th edition celebrated as a milestone season
